steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses requiring separation
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses: "A team... collected arthropods with [blank]" and "another research team... gathered them with bug vacuums." These must be properly separated by punctuation.

steps.title: Match punctuation to the parenthetical transitional phrase
steps.content: The blank must contain "sweep nets" plus the parenthetical phrase "for instance" (meaning "as an instance/example"), which describes sweep nets as an example of a collection method. When this phrase ends the first independent clause, it takes a comma before it, and a semicolon separates the two clauses: "sweep nets, for instance;" This structure properly uses the semicolon rule for independent clauses while integrating the parenthetical transition. The other options either reverse this punctuation, create a comma splice, or omit punctuation entirely. That matches choice A — A is correct.
